title: Tip of the week #5: Use dashboards to visualise your projects
excerpt: Dashboards are a great way to get a snapshot of all the various activities and projects occurring on all your different boards. They present real-time data on any individual aspect of the project you’re looking to analys…

posts.author: arubinomunoz (Amalia Rubino @Upscale (part of The Adaptavist Group))
posts.cookedHtml:
Dashboards are a great way to get a snapshot of all the various activities and projects occurring on all your different boards. They present real-time data on any individual aspect of the project you’re looking to analyse.

For example, you can use dashboards to automatically gather and present high-level project data for insights on budgets, goals, schedules, resources, and more!

Get instant overviews on every aspect of your project.1400×674 90.7 KB

posts.author: max64lowes (max)
posts.cookedHtml:
arubinomunoz:

Dashboards are a great way to get a snapshot of all the various activities and projects occurring on all your different boards. They present real-time data on any individual aspect of the project you’re looking to analyse.

For example, you can use dashboards to automatically gather and present high-level project data for insights on budgets, goals, schedules, resources, and more!

Dashboards offer a powerful snapshot of all your activities and projects across multiple boards, bringing everything together in one view. They deliver real-time data, helping you quickly analyse any aspect of a project—whether it’s budgets, goals, schedules, resources, or more. By automatically gathering and presenting high-level insights, dashboards make it easier to monitor progress, spot trends, and make informed decisions. peryourhealth
